```json
[
  {
    "idea": "EV Charger Network Expansion Momentum",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",
    "data": {
      "source": "OpenChargeMap API daily charger count by region",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Rapid growth in public EV charging infrastructure signals increased capex and demand for electric vehicle adoption, indicating macro tailwinds for industrials and materials.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLI",
      "rationale": "EV charger buildout requires metals, electrical infrastructure, and industrial construction—direct XLI exposure."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly net new chargers added exceeds 1500 units (7-day rolling average) above the prior month's median",
      "exit": "After 10 trading days or when weekly charger additions drop below 1200 units",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Regional charger buildout fluctuates seasonally and by policy; multiple regions typically add >1500 chargers per week during growth cycles."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Jobless Claims Shock Reversal Trade",
    "family": "labor_jobs",
    "data": {
      "source": "FRED series ICSA (initial jobless claims weekly)",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When initial jobless claims spike above 2-standard-deviations of the 8-week rolling mean, a mean-reversion bounce typically follows within 2-3 weeks as temporary layoffs resolve.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Claims shock reversals signal labor market stabilization, boosting consumer discretionary spending confidence."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "When weekly initial claims exceed (8-week rolling mean + 2*stdev) AND prior week was not already elevated",
      "exit": "After claims return to rolling mean or 12 trading days elapse",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Weekly claims data is volatile; seasonal layoffs and temporary shocks occur 3-5 times per year, triggering mean-reversion signals."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Weather Shock Agricultural Input Demand Spike",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",
    "data": {
      "source": "Open-Meteo daily precipitation and temperature anomalies for major US agricultural regions",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Severe drought (>2 weeks below -1.5σ precipitation) or unexpected freeze events trigger emergency demand for crop inputs, fertilizers, and irrigation—driving materials and industrial stocks.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLB",
      "rationale": "Ag input shocks drive demand for fertilizers, chemicals, and equipment—core XLB constituents."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If 5-day rolling precipitation anomaly for Corn Belt drops below -1.5σ OR temperature swings >8°C below seasonal norm for 3+ consecutive days",
      "exit": "After 8 trading days or when weather normalizes toward seasonal mean",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Weather volatility is frequent; drought/freeze shocks occur 4-6 times per growing season across US regions."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Earthquake Damage Insurance Claim Anticipation",
    "family": "local_economy_weirdness",
    "data": {
      "source": "USGS Earthquake Activity API (daily magnitude >4.0 events in insurable regions)",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Significant earthquake clusters (>3 events magnitude 4.0+ within 7 days in same region) trigger insurance loss recognition and reconstruction demand—benefiting financials and industrials.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLF",
      "rationale": "Immediate insurance claim spikes hurt financial sector valuations; property & casualty insurers face margin pressure."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If 7-day rolling count of magnitude 4.0+ earthquakes in CA/OR/WA exceeds 3 events",
      "exit": "After 15 trading days or when seismic activity normalizes to <1 event per week",
      "expected_frequency": "high"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Earthquake clusters occur regularly along US fault lines; 2-3 trading opportunities per quarter in seismically active states."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Google Trends Surge in 'Unemployment Benefits' Search Volume",
    "family": "consumer_stress",
    "data": {
      "source": "Google Trends weekly search interest for 'unemployment benefits' and 'how to apply for unemployment'",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Sharp week-over-week spikes in unemployment benefits search volume (>40% increase) precede negative consumer sentiment and spending cuts, signaling economic stress.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Consumer discretionary spending declines as households anticipate or experience job loss."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly search volume index exceeds prior 4-week average by >40% AND prior week was not already elevated",
      "exit": "After 10 trading days or when search volume returns to 4-week baseline",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Google Trends search spikes correlate with labor market weakness; seasonal layoffs and recession fears trigger 3-4 signals per year."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Airline Load Factor Compression Signal",
    "family": "travel_mobility",
    "data": {
      "source": "BTS Airline Load Factor monthly average (via html_table scrape)",
      "type": "scrape",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When monthly load factor drops >2% from prior month, airlines cut capacity, reducing fuel demand and signaling travel demand weakness—bearish for airlines and energy.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLE",
      "rationale": "Lower airline load factors reduce jet fuel consumption and signal reduced consumer travel spending."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If latest month's load factor is >2 percentage points below prior month AND below 12-month rolling average",
      "exit": "After 15 trading days or when load factor recovers toward 12-month mean",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "medium",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Seasonal capacity adjustments occur quarterly; holiday travel shifts and recession fears trigger load factor compressions 3-4 times per year."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Corporate Debt Maturity Wall RSS Count Surge",
    "family": "attention_sentiment",
    "data": {
      "source": "RSS feeds from financial news (Bloomberg, Reuters, WSJ) filtered for 'debt maturity' and 'refinance risk' mentions",
      "type": "scrape",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When daily RSS mentions of corporate debt maturity risks spike >3x the rolling 30-day mean, media focus on refinancing pressure builds, signaling near-term credit stress and equity weakness.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLF",
      "rationale": "Debt refinancing anxiety pressures financial sector valuations and bank credit portfolios."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If daily RSS count for 'debt maturity' OR 'refinance risk' exceeds 3x the 30-day rolling median",
      "exit": "After 7 trading days or when RSS mention count returns to rolling baseline",
      "expected_frequency": "high"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Debt walls and refinancing cycles trigger news surges 2-3 times per quarter; Q1 and Q4 earnings-driven volatility ensures frequent signals."
  },
  {
    "idea": "EIA Natural Gas Storage Build Momentum Divergence",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",
    "data": {
      "source": "EIA Weekly Natural Gas Inventory Data (via FRED or EIA API)",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When weekly gas storage builds accelerate >20% faster than the prior 4-week rolling average (suggesting oversupply), natural gas prices compress, hurting energy producer margins—bearish energy.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLE",
      "rationale": "Rapid storage accumulation signals weak demand or oversupply, driving down nat gas prices and producer revenue."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly storage build exceeds 4-week rolling average by >20% AND prior 2 weeks showed accelerating builds",
      "exit": "After 10 trading days or when weekly build rate normalizes below 4-week mean",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Seasonal gas storage cycles and weather-driven demand shifts trigger build acceleration signals 8-12 times per year."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Hospital Admission Rate RSS Spike (Non-COVID)",
    "family": "local_economy_weirdness",
    "data": {
      "source": "RSS feeds from health department and hospital network news mentioning 'admission surge' or 'bed occupancy'",
      "type": "scrape",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Sudden spikes in RSS mentions of hospital admission surges (>2x rolling average) signal seasonal flu, RSV, or accident clusters, driving healthcare utilization and equipment demand.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLV",
      "rationale": "Hospital admission surges boost pharmaceutical demand, diagnostic testing, and medical device utilization—benefiting healthcare sector."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If daily RSS count for hospital admission-related terms exceeds 2x the 20-day rolling median",
      "exit": "After 8 trading days or when RSS mention count returns to rolling baseline",
      "expected_frequency": "high"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Seasonal flu/RSV surges occur Oct-Mar annually; unexpected accident clusters and disease spikes trigger signals 4-6 times per year."
  },
  {
    "idea": "FRED Core CPI Expectation Reversal Divergence",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",
    "data": {
      "source": "FRED series MMNRNJ (5-year inflation expectation) weekly aggregation",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When 5-year inflation expectations drop >15 basis points in a single week after 4+ weeks of stable/rising expectations, it signals a demand destruction shock or disinflation surprise—bullish for long-duration assets.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLK",
      "rationale": "Collapsing inflation expectations lower discount rates and boost tech/growth valuations."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly change in 5-year inflation expectations is <-15bps AND prior 4 weeks showed stable/positive change",
      "exit": "After 12 trading days or when expectations stabilize/rebound >10bps",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "medium",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Inflation expectations shift with Fed policy and CPI surprises; directional reversals occur 3-4 times per quarter during volatile periods."
  }
]
```
